title: The One Where the Monkey Gets Away

lines.text: {Transcriber's Note: The credits list two characters, Tia and Samantha, who I assume are the sweaty women Joey and Chandler meet. However, I don't know which is which, so I've simply called them Woman #1 and Woman #2.}
Central Perk, Rachel is talking to a customer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, okay, I checked. We have: Earl Grey, English Breakfast, Cinnamon Stick, Camomile, Mint Medley, Blackberry, and.. oh, wait, there's one more, um.. Lemon Soother. You're not the guy that asked for the tea, are you? (Guy shakes his head) Okay.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Monica enters with some mail.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Mail call, Rachel Green, bunk seven.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. (Examines it) Oh, cool! Free sample of coffee!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good! 'Cause where else would we get any?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Right. ...Oh great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Country club newsletter. My mother sends me the engagement notices for 'inspiration.' Oh my God! Oh my God, it's Barry and Mindy!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Barry who you almost...?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Barry who I almost.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And Mindy, your maid of...?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mindy, my maid of. Oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Takes it) That's Mindy? Wow, she is pretty. (Sees Rachel's look) Lucky. To have had a friend like you.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel and Ross are eating Chinese.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel. Bring me the rice, c'mon. Bring me the rice, c'mon. Good boy. Good boy. C'mere, gimme the rice. (Marcel brings the rice) Thank you, good boy. Well, I see he's finally mastered the difference between 'bring me the' and 'pee in the'. (Rachel ignores him) 'Bring me the' and- Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Oh, this is so stupid! I mean, I gave Barry up, right? I should be happy for them! I am, I'm happy for them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Oh, oh, I guess it would be different if I were- with somebody.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoah, uh, what happened to, uh, 'Forget relationships! I'm done with men!' The whole, uh, penis embargo?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I don't know. I guess it's not about no guys, it's about the right guy, y'know? I mean, with Barry, it was safe and it was easy, but there was no heat. With Paolo, that's all there was, was heat! And it was just this raw, animal, sexual...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait-wait. I, I got it. I was there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I mean, do you think you can ever have both? Y'know? Someone who's like, who's like your best friend, but then also can make your toes curl?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes. Yes. Yes! Yes, I really do! In fact, it's funny, very often, someone who you wouldn't think could-could curl your toes, might just be the one who...
Enter the other four

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...Gets interrupted. Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, how was the movie?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So good!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Suck-fest.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Toootal chick-flick.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I'm sorry it wasn't one of those movies with, like, y'know, guns and bombs and, like, buses going really fast...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I don't need violence to enjoy a movie. Just so long as there's a little nudity.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There was nudity!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I meant female nudity. Alright? I don't need to see Lou Grant frolicking.

lines.speaker: Monica and Phoebe
lines.text: Hugh! Hugh Grant!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright, I've gotta go. C'mon, Marcel! C'mon! We're gonna go take a bath. Yes we are, aren't we? Yes, we are.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They're still just friends, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Marcel) And I will see you tomorrow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right, you're gonna spend tomorrow at Aunt Rachel's, aren't you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, hang on, hang on. Does Aunt Monica get a say in this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'Pwease, Aunt Monica, pwease?' Oh, unclench. You're not even gonna be there.
Joe-G's Pizza, the guys are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe we are even having this discussion.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I agree. I'm, like, in disbelief.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I mean, don't you think if things were gonna happen with Rachel, they would've happened already?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm telling you, she said she's looking for a relationship with someone exactly like me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She really said that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I added the 'exactly like me' part... But she said she's looking for someone, and someone is gonna be there tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 'Tonight' tonight?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I think it's perfect. Y'know, it's just gonna be the two of us, she spent all day taking care of my monkey...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't remember the last time I got a girl to take care of my monkey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Anyway, I figured after work I'd go pick up a bottle of wine, go over there and, uh, try to woo her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, y'know what you should do? You should take her back to the 1890's, when that phrase was last used.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is taking care of Marcel and they are watching a soap opera.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now, now the one in the feather boa, that's Dr. Francis. Now, she used to be a man. Okay, now look, see, there's Raven. We hate her. We're glad she's dying. Okay- (Marcel pushes down a cushion to reveal a shoe) Wh- wh- Marcel, are you playing with Monica's shoes? You know you're not supposed to pl- whoah. Marcel, did you poo in the shoe? (Takes the shoe into the kitchen) Marcel, bad monkey! Oh! Oh! (She notices the newsletter and taps the contents of the shoes onto it, then folds it shut) Sorry, Barry. Little engagement gift. I'm sure you didn't register for that. (She leaves the apartment holding the newsletter at arm's length. However, she leaves the door open. Marcel runs out in the opposite direction. There is a shot from the TV and Rachel runs back in) Who died? Who died? Roll him over! Oh, c'mon, roll him over! Oh...! Well, we know it wasn't Dexter, right Marcel? Because- (Looks down and notices he is missing) Marcel? Marc- (Notices the open door)
[Time lapse. Now everyone but Ross and Phoebe is back at Monica and Rachel's.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How could you lose him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know. We were watching TV, and then he pooped in Monica's shoe-

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait. He pooped in my shoe? Which one?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know. The left one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Which ones?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh, those little clunky Amish things you think go with everything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whoah, ooh, why is the air in here so negative?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Rachel lost Marcel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, how?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He- he pooped in my shoe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Which one?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Those cute little black ones I wear all the time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, which one? The right or left? 'Cause the left one is lucky...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: C'mon, you guys, what're we gonna do, what're we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright alright. You're a monkey. You're loose in the city. Where do you go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, it's his first time out, so he's probably gonna wanna do some of the touristy things. I'll go to Cats, you go to the Russian Tea Room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, my, God, c'mon, you guys! He's gonna be home any minute! He's gonna kill me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, we'll start with the building. You guys take the first and second floor, Phoebe and I'll take third and fourth.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what'm I gonna do? What'm I gonna do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, you stay here, and just wait by the phone. Spray Lysol in my shoe, and wait for Ross to kill you.
They all leave

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Anybody wanna trade? Oh...
[Cut to a hallway in the building, Monica and Phoebe are knocking on a door. Mr. Heckles emerges.]

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: Whaddyou want?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Mr. Heckles, our friend lost a monkey. Have you seen it?

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: I left a Belgian waffle out here, did you take it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why would you leave your Belgian waffle in the hall?

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: I wasn't ready for it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: A monkey. Have you seen a monkey?

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: Saw Regis Philbin once...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, thank you, Mr. Heckles. (They move off)

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: You owe me a waffle.
[Cut to Monica and Rachel's.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (On the phone) Okay, he's a, he's a black capuchian monkey with a white face... (Enter Ross) ...with, with Russian dressing and, and pickles on the side. Okay. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey. How did, uh, how'd it go today?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great! It went great. Really great. Hey, is that wine?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. You, uh, you want some?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I would love some. But y'know what? Y'know what? Let's not drink it here. I'm feeling kinda crazy. You wanna go to Newark?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, okay, yeah, we could do that, but before we head off to the murder capital of the North-East, I was, uh, kinda wanting to run something by you. Y'know how we were, uh, y'know, talking before about, uh, relationships and stuff? (Uncorks the wine) Well-

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God, Ross, I cannot do this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, quick and painful. (Starts to cork the wine)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God... Okay. Alright. Alright. Okay. Ross, please don't hate me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, what? What-what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know Marcel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I kind of... I kind of lost him.
[Cut to outside the window, with Ross reacting with disbelief. The shot pans back until we see Marcel sitting on the window ledge.]
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Angry) I- I- I ca- I can't believe this. I mean, all I asked you to do was keep him in the apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I know, I'm sorry-

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, y'know what, I guess it's partially my fault. Y'know, I shouldn't've, uh, asked you to start off with a monkey. I should've started you off with like a pen or a pencil.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Tearfully) Ross, I'm doing everything that I can, I've got everybody looking for him, and I- (Door buzzer goes and she runs to get it) Oh! Who is it?

lines.speaker: Intercom
lines.text: Animal Control.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: See? I've even called Animal Control!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You called Animal Control?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh... why... do you not like them?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel is an illegal exotic animal. I'm not allowed to have him in the city. If they find him, they'll take him away from me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: O-okay, now see, you never ever ever told us that...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right, I.. 'cause I didn't expect you were gonna invite them to the apartment!
A knock on the door. Rachel swiftly opens it

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, thanks for coming.

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: (Animal Control) Somebody called about a monkey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y'know what? That was a complete misunderstanding! (Ross puts his arms around her and they act all sweetness and light)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, we thought we had a monkey, but we-we didn't.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Turned out it was a hat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Cat! What'm I saying? Cat!
Luisa nods, but then Monica and Phoebe run in

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi. We checked the third and fourth floor, no-one's seen Marcel.

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My uncle Marcel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, is that who the monkey's named after?

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Oookay. Are you aware that possession of an illegal exotic is, uh, punishable by up to two years in prison and confiscation of the animal?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God. You'd put that poor little creature in jail?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pheebs, you remember how we talked about saying things quietly to yourself first?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, but there isn't always time!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look. I'm sure there's some friendly way to reconcile this! Um, have a seat. First of all, we haven't been introduced, I'm Monica Geller.

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Oh my God, you are! And you're Rachel Green!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Luisa Gianetti! Lincoln High? I sat behind you guys in home room!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Luisa? Oh my God! Monica! It's Luisa!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The Luisa from home room!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: You have no idea who I am, do you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, none at all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: None.

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Well, maybe that's because you spent four years ignoring me. I mean, would it have been so hard to say 'Morning, Luisa'? Or 'Nice overalls'?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I'm- I'm so sorry!

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Ah, it's not so much you, you were fat, you had your own problems. (To Rachel) But you? What a bitch!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Be that as it may, d'you think you could just help us out here on that monkey thing? Y'know, just for old times' sake? Go Bobcats?

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: I could... but I won't. If I find that monkey, he's mine. (Leaves)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Dun-dun-duuuur! Sorry.
[Cut to another part of the building. We see Marcel jump in through a window and run down some stairs, then Chandler and Joey come down from the upper floor without noticing.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Marcel?
They come to a door and silently agree to try it. A very sweaty woman emerges
Woman No. 1: Hi, can I help you?
Chandler and Joey are dumbstruck for a moment

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Um, we're kind of having an emergency and we-we were looking for something...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A monkey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes have you seen any?
Woman No. 1: No. No, haven't seen a monkey. Do you know anything about fixing radiators?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Um, sure! Did you, uh, did you try turning the knob back the other way?
Woman No. 1: Of course.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Then, no.
Another sweaty woman comes to the door and speaks to her friend
Woman No. 2: Did I put too much rum in here?
Joey and Chandler shoot each other glances
Woman No. 1: Just a sec. (To Chandler and Joey) Hope you find your monkey. (She starts to shut the door

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, nononowaitwaitwaitnono! Uh... we may not know anything about radiators per se, but we do have a certain amount of expertise in the heating and cooling... mileu.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, aren't we kind of in the middle of something here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but these women are very hot, and they need our help! And they're very hot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We can't, alright? (To the women) We're sorry. You have no idea how sorry, but... We promised we'd find this monkey. If you see him, he's about yea high and answers to the name Marcel, so if we could get some pictures of you, you'd really be helping us out.
The women quickly shut the door

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, from now on, you don't get to talk to other people.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Marcel?!
[Cut to Monica and Phoebe searching the basement.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh-my-God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Whaaat!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Something just brushed up against my right leg!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's okay, it was just my left leg.
Marcel makes a monkeyish noise. He is sitting in the corner

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Oh, c'mere, Marcel! Oh, Marcel, c'mere!
Luisa appears on the stairs

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Step aside, ladies! (She loads a gun)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What're you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Just a small tranquiliser.
In slow motion we see Phoebe look at Marcel, then at Luisa. She jumps toward Marcel just as Luisa fires the gun.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Run, Marcel, run! Run, Marcel! (Marcel runs off and Luisa runs after him. Monica goes to check up on Phoebe) Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, think so. Oh! (She notices the tranquiliser dart has hit her in the butt and removes it) Huh. (Sways back) Whoah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh gosh.

[Cut to Marcel walking along a hallway. He notices a banana on the floor and picks it up. The hand of an unseen person grabs him and carries him away. Then cut to Ross and Rachel on the street outside.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Marcel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marc- oh, this is ridiculous! We've been all over the neighbourhood. He's gone, he's-he's just gone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you don't know that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh come on. It's cold, it's dark, he doesn't know the Village. (Kicks a sign in frustration) And now I have a broken foot. I have no monkey, and a broken foot! Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I said I'm sorry like a million times. What do you want me to do? You want me to break my foot too? Okay, I'm gonna break my foot, right here. (Kicks the sign) Ow!! Oh! Oh my God, oh my God! There, are you happy now?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. Y'know, now that you kicked the sign, hey! I don't miss Marcel any more!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, it is not like I did this on purpose.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no no no. Nono, this is just vintage Rachel. I mean, things just sort of happen around you. I mean, you're off in Rachel-land, doing your Rachel-thing, totally oblivious to people's monkeys, or to people's feelings...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't even wanna hear it, you're just...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, forget it, okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What?
A man carrying a box of bananas walks past them. They stare for a minute and then hobble after him

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: Hey! Hey, Bananaman!
Scene 4: Everyone in the hall outside Mr. Heckles' door. Ross is carrying the box of bananas. He bangs on the door

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this is so intense. One side of my butt is totally asleep, and the other side has no idea.
Mr. Heckles opens the door

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi, did you order some bananas?

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: What about it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gimme back my monkey.

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: I don't have a monkey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Then what's with all the bananas?

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: Potassium.
There is a monkey-like noise from within and Ross pushes past Mr. Heckles and enters his apartment

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel? Marcel? Okay, where is he? Where is he? Marcel? Marcel?
Marcel jumps into view wearing a pink dress. Everybody gasps

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel! What've you done to him?

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: That's my monkey. That's Patti, Patti the monkey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you insane? C'mere, Marcel, c'mon. (Marcel starts to go to him)

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: C'mere, Patti. (Marcel turns round)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: C'mere, Marcel. (Turns to Ross)

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: C'mere, Patti. (Turns to Mr. Heckles)

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: (Out of shot) Here, monkey. Here, monkey! Here, monkey! (Marcel runs to the door and into Luisa's cage, which she slams shut) Gotcha.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, gimme my monkey back.

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: That's my monkey.

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: You're both gonna have to take this up with the judge.

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: That's not my monkey. Just the dress is mine, you can send that back whenever.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright, I want my monkey.

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, c'mon, Luisa!

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Sorry, prom queen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel) You had to be a bitch in high school, you couldn't've been fat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright. In high school I was the prom queen and I was the homecoming queen and the class president and you... were also there! But if you take this monkey, I will lose one of the most important people in my life. You can hate me if you want, but please do not punish him. C'mon, Luisa, you have a chance to be the bigger person here! Take it!

lines.speaker: Luisa
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright. Well then how about I call your supervisor, and I tell her that you shot my friend in the ass with a dart?
Monica and Rachel's. Rachel and Ross are there. Ross is trying to get the dress off Marcel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It'll be nice to get this off finally, won't it? Yes it will. (Marcel resists) Or we can leave it on for now, that's fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, with the right pair of pumps, that would be a great little outfit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, I'm- I'm sorry I was so hard on you before, it's just I...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Ross, c'mon. It's my fault, I almost lost your...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but you were the one who got him back, y'know? You, you were great. ...Hey, we uh, we still have that, uh, that bottle of wine. You in the mood for, uh, something grape?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That'd be good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright. (He goes to get the glasses. Then he hesitates and turns off the main light. Rachel looks round and he acts surprised) The, uh, the neighbours must be vacuuming. (He sits down and starts to pour the wine) Well, so long as we're here and, uh, not on the subject, I was thinking about, uh, how mad we got at each other before, and, um, I was thinking maybe it was partially because of how we, um...
Barry bursts in

lines.speaker: Barry
lines.text: Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Barry?!

lines.speaker: Barry
lines.text: I can't. I can't do it, I can't marry Mindy. I think I'm still in love with you.

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We have got to start locking that door!
Closing Credits
Central Perk, Monica, Joey, Phoebe, and Chandler are looking through Monica's high school yearbook

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is me in The Sound of Music. See the von Trapp kids?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's because I'm in front of them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh. I thought that was an alp.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, high school was not my favourite time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I dunno, I loved high school. Y'know? It was just four years of parties and dating and sex.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well I went to boarding school with four hundred boys. Any sex I had would've involved a major lifestyle choice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Gosh, doesn't it seem like a million years ago?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Oooh! Ooh! Ooh! (She stands up and starts to dance around) Ooh! My butt cheek is waking up! Oooh! Ooh!
End
